---
title: "Sampling"
format: html
author: Anthony Gao
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
#| label: setup
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.data)
```

```{r}
#| label: tibble
shaming
```

```{r}
#| label: working
#| cache: true
set.seed(10)

samples <- tibble(replicate = 1:1000) %>% 
  mutate(sample = map(replicate, ~ slice_sample(shaming, n = 50))) %>% 
  mutate(sample_mean = map_dbl(sample, ~ mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)),
         sample_se = map_dbl(sample, ~ sd(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(.x$age)))),
         lower = sample_mean - 1.96 * sample_se,
         upper = sample_mean + 1.96 * sample_se) %>% 
  select(-sample)
```

```{r}
truth <- mean(shaming$age, na.rm = TRUE)
mean(samples$lower < truth & samples$upper > truth)
```

```{r}
samples_100 <- samples %>% 
  slice_head(n = 100) %>% 
  mutate(id = row_number())

ggplot(samples_100, aes(x = sample_mean, y = id)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = truth, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "95% Confidence Intervals from 100 Random Samples",
    subtitle = "Approximately 95% of sample confidence intervals capture the true population mean",
    caption = "Source: primer.data",
    x = "Sample Mean Age (Years)",
    y = "Sample Draw Number"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
#| label: biased_working
set.seed(10)

shaming_weighted <- shaming %>% 
  mutate(weight = ifelse(age > 40, 3, 1))

biased_samples <- tibble(replicate = 1:1000) %>% 
  mutate(sample = map(replicate, ~ slice_sample(shaming_weighted, n = 50, weight_by = weight))) %>% 
  mutate(sample_mean = map_dbl(sample, ~ mean(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE)),
         sample_se = map_dbl(sample, ~ sd(.x$age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(.x$age)))),
         lower = sample_mean - 1.96 * sample_se,
         upper = sample_mean + 1.96 * sample_se) %>% 
  select(-sample)
```

```{r}
mean(biased_samples$lower < truth & biased_samples$upper > truth)

ggplot(biased_samples, aes(x = sample_mean)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = truth, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(biased_samples$sample_mean), color = "black", linetype = "solid") +
  labs(
    title = "Density of Biased Sample Means",
    subtitle = "The average of the biased sample means (black solid line) is shifted away from the truth (red dashed line)",
    caption = "Source: primer.data",
    x = "Sample Mean Age (Years)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
